DukesShpitserTchetgen2023: completeness is the proximal mediation identification condition
DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024: discrete causal queries reduce to constrained polynomial programs yielding sharp bounds

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1, no-proxy fallback and proxy tightening comparison

Sjolander2009: randomized-treatment natural-direct-effect bounds under mediator--outcome confounding

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy_v1, Conjecture 2: strict proxy tightening over no-proxy bounds

published_prior_art.cite: GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 gives no-completeness proxy bounds, while BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 gives the no-proxy mediation floor; neither states this exact restricted certificate
BaiWuSunCui2026 and WuBaiCui2026 give bridge-based path-specific identification; ZhangShanLi2026 is a closer omitted proxy treatment-induced-confounding comparator

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 (robust strict contraction)
novelty_flags.one_line: This is a restricted re-labelling of pid_mediation_proxy_v1, Conjecture 2 (“strict proxy tightening over no-proxy bounds”), which already asks for weak/incomplete proxies to exclude no-proxy natural-effect endpoints.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 2 (recanting extension)
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses ZhangShanLi2026, which uses proxies for unmeasured treatment-induced confounders in mediation and is closer to this recanting-witness setup than the listed bridge-identification papers.

soundness_flags.label: Open-ended Question 2 / def:rw-targets / def:rw-feasible-set
soundness_flags.one_line: The recanting identified sets quantify over \(\lambda^{\mathrm{rw}}\in\mathcal F^{\mathrm{rw}}\) although that feasible set contains \((\lambda^{\mathrm{rw}},q^Z,q^W)\), and no positive-cell nonempty recanting domain is defined to rule out vacuous endpoint exclusion.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The result is conditional on the declared three-state, positive-mass, factorized shared-coarsening SCM and its separation condition.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any interval contraction is only for \(\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}\), not unrestricted latent-proxy mediation models.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The delegated path-specific question is conditional on the declared recanting-witness graph and all three failed-completeness restrictions.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any resulting contraction would concern only the repaired finite recanting-witness compatible-SCM fiber.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Pivot the primary kernel to a causal target or a proved strict-extension characterization demonstrably outside pid_mediation_proxy_v1, rather than another existence certificate for strict weak-proxy tightening.

If retaining the recanting branch, define a nonempty positive-cell recanting domain and quantify \(\Theta^{\mathrm{rw}}\) over full feasible triples; then position it against ZhangShanLi2026 as well as BaiWuSunCui2026 and WuBaiCui2026.
Add an explicit theorem showing why the shared-coarsening three-state factorized class is not covered by the earlier proxy-null response-function proposal before claiming it as a distinct kernel.

recommended_next_step: Pivot away from the already-catalogued strict-proxy-tightening kernel; only retain the recanting branch after repairing its feasible-set typing and rebuilding its novelty case.
